Generated SDK consumes public contract pack only.
Conformance runner validates public and internal boundary contracts.
Conformance runner checks generated SDK boundary assertions.
Ingress preflight and submit paths require authority checks.
Promotion decisions require authority decisions.
Materialization can only use accepted/in-force promoted basis.
Query can read governed materializations through service boundary.
Output assembly may require query basis through public query path.
Candidate imports require identity resolution and ambiguity preservation.
Calculation previews emit numeric traces.

forbiddenImports.reason: SDK must call public materialization surface, not service internals.
SDK must not invoke promotion internals.
SDK must call public authority/preflight surface only.
Sample apps must use SDK/import public surface only.
Publication may use governed basis/queries, not direct truth-store mutation.
Query service must not create hidden truth stores.
Candidate imports must not bypass governed promotion/review.
Calculation service must not let packs mutate core quantity meaning.

rules: Apps import SDK packages only; they do not import platform service internals.
SDK imports generated public contracts only; it does not import internal schema catalog or service modules.
Services may use runtime-core and contracts, but all state-affecting cross-service actions must travel through declared ports.
Materialization writes are internal to materialization service and must be derived from accepted/in-force basis only.
Adapter-service emits candidate imports and loss maps; it must not call promotion internals directly.
Trace-service provides trace retrieval; trace output is explanation, not accepted truth.
